comprehension_gate.instruction:
READ THIS ENTIRE PROMPT before posting your comprehension gate. Then READ every file listed in required_attachments. Then POST a comprehension gate comment on Vikunja Task 82, prefixed:
[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]
Your comprehension comment MUST contain ALL of the following sections (verbatim section headers required, in this exact order, no numbered prefixes):
A. MILESTONE OBJECTIVE (in your own words, 3-5 sentences) B. WORK ITEMS (one sentence each for ALL 10 WIs — do NOT summarize, do NOT group) C. FILES TO CREATE (list every new test file exactly as specified in deliverable) D. FILES TO MODIFY (list every existing file you will add tests to) E. FILES TO READ (all production source files you will read for context) F. DELIVERABLE STRUCTURE (recite VERBATIM: branch name, every test file with its test class/function names and naming conventions, ledger entry) G. ORACLE EXPECTATION (recite VERBATIM the exact git diff --name-only grep command and the expected EMPTY output) H. MATURE-NOT-MINIMAL 1-HOUR CAP ACKNOWLEDGMENT (one sentence) I. RISKS AND AMBIGUITIES (list any; at minimum address: mock-controlled centroids and embeddings strategy for SR dual-gate, AO config-validation floor of 6/13, pgov_display.hide() test lives in ui_shell/tests not AO tests) J. PRODUCTION FILE PROHIBITION ACKNOWLEDGMENT (quote the prohibition verbatim)
After posting, STOP. Do NOT proceed to implementation. You cannot begin code work until you have posted your [agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension] comment AND received [agent:sdo][phase:comprehension-review] VERDICT: APPROVED from the SDO.
If SDO issues VERDICT: REVISE, update your comprehension gate and wait again.

milestone.objective: Close all test coverage, assertion-quality, and boundary-verification gaps in the assistant_orchestrator (AO) and semantic_router (SR) service clusters identified by the Sprint 7 audit in docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md. This EA addresses: the AO PGOV leakage threshold exact-point boundary (cosine_similarity == 0.85); the SR dual-gate exact-point boundaries (similarity == 0.50, margin == 0.04, margin == 0.03) via mock-controlled centroids and embeddings; AO entrypoint.py config-validation coverage (≥ 6 of the ~13 uncovered TOML constraints, all 13 preferred under mature-not-minimal); AO entrypoint.py HEARTBEAT message handling; AO entrypoint.py stop() isolation; circuit_breaker.py over-limit-token, simultaneous-trip, and new_request() reset paths; pgov.py CREDIT_CARD and HEX_SECRET PII pattern tests; direct constants assertion test files for both AO and SR; and the pgov_display.py hide() assignment-posing-as-assertion bug fix. No production code is modified. All work is pure test-authoring.
milestone.ledger_entry_note: SDO reserved entry 52 at authoring time. Sprint 9 EA-1 consumed entry 51 in a parallel merge window; EA-2 should therefore verify the next free entry at commit time and use whichever is free (target 52; adjust upward if advanced). Record the actual entry number in the commit message and completion report.
milestone.depends_on: EA-1 merged to main. Branch feature/p5-task8-ea1-policy-agent-hardening must be present in main history. If EA-1 is not yet merged at EA pickup, STOP and post a blocker comment — sequential execution is mandatory per SDV §5.2 and Sprint 8 out-of-scope policy.

item.source: TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md §Boundary Violations — AO pgov.py — Exact PGOV Leakage Threshold (0.85) Untested
item.title: Add AO PGOV leakage threshold exact-point boundary test (cosine_similarity == 0.85)
item.detail:
File: services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_pgov_boundaries.py (CREATE NEW) Class: TestPGOVLeakageThresholdBoundary
Add test: test_leakage_cosine_similarity_at_threshold_rejects Construct PGOVPipeline with PGOV_COSINE_THRESHOLD=0.85 (the production constant). Inject a mocked leakage detector whose check_leakage.return_value == 0.85 exactly. Feed a minimal chunk set (one chunk is sufficient; read test_pgov.py TestPGOVPipeline::test_leakage_with_injected_detector for the existing pattern). Run the PGOV pipeline. Assert: result.approved is False. Assert: the leakage-reason code matches the production denial path (read services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py to identify the exact reason string; do NOT invent a code).
Rationale: services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py uses a comparison operator on cosine_similarity against PGOV_COSINE_THRESHOLD. Existing tests cover 0.92 (above) and 0.50 (below) but NOT the exact boundary at 0.85. A typo changing `>=` to `>` (or vice versa) at the threshold comparison would pass all existing tests while silently flipping the boundary classification.
Read before writing: services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py (PGOVPipeline, leakage path) services/assistant_orchestrator/src/constants.py (PGOV_COSINE_THRESHOLD == 0.85) services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_pgov.py (TestPGOVPipeline::test_leakage_with_injected_detector pattern) services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_pgov.py (TestPGOVPipeline::test_leakage_below_threshold_passes pattern)
Mocking strategy: inject a detector via the same seam already used by test_leakage_with_injected_detector. Do not add new production hooks.

item.title: Add SR dual-gate exact-point boundary tests (similarity == 0.50, margin == 0.04, margin == 0.03)
item.detail:
File: services/semantic_router/tests/test_dual_gate_thresholds.py (CREATE NEW) Class: TestSemanticRouterDualGateThresholds
Add three tests targeting the exact boundary points of the SR dual-gate:
test_similarity_at_confidence_threshold_passes_or_escalates: Mock-control the router's centroids so the top similarity equals 0.50 exactly (CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD from services/semantic_router/src/constants.py). Provide an embedding that yields cosine similarity == 0.50 to the top centroid and a second centroid that preserves the margin gate (e.g., similarity <= 0.46). Classify and assert the behavior at the exact threshold matches the production comparison operator (read router.py to determine whether 0.50 is inclusive; the test asserts the CURRENT production behavior, not an assumed one).
test_margin_at_exact_gate_classifies: Mock-control centroids so top-1 similarity minus top-2 similarity == 0.04 exactly (CONFIDENCE_MARGIN from constants.py). Assert the classifier's behavior at the exact margin (inclusive vs. exclusive — read router.py to determine).
test_margin_just_below_gate_escalates_or_rejects: Same setup as above but with margin == 0.03 (one hundredth below the gate). Assert the classifier does NOT return a confident classification — i.e. it escalates, returns a None/ambiguous label, or matches whatever the production under-margin path does (read router.py).
Read before writing: services/semantic_router/src/router.py (classify() or equivalent; dual-gate logic) services/semantic_router/src/constants.py (CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD == 0.50, CONFIDENCE_MARGIN == 0.04) services/semantic_router/src/intents.py (centroid structures, if relevant) services/semantic_router/tests/test_router.py (existing mocking patterns)
Mocking strategy: monkeypatch or construct the router with injected centroids and an injected embedding function so the similarity values are deterministic. The audit explicitly flagged that dual-gate boundary behavior cannot be tested at exact values without controlled centroids and embeddings; use whatever seam the existing router.py exposes. If no clean seam exists and patching requires touching production code, STOP and report the blocker — do NOT add a production hook.

item.source: TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md §Coverage Map — AO entrypoint.py — Config Validation Floor
item.title: Add AO entrypoint.py config-validation coverage (≥ 6 of ~13 uncovered constraints; all 13 preferred)
item.detail:
File: services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_entrypoint.py (MODIFY) Class: TestAssistantOrchestratorConfigValidation (CREATE NEW CLASS in existing file)
Add parametrized or per-constraint tests for the TOML config-validation constraints currently uncovered. Only `deployment_mode` and `response_depth_mode` are covered at HEAD 29cea32. The full list of uncovered constraints (per audit §Coverage Map):
1. device MUST equal "GPU" (ADR-011 hardening) 2. priority RANGE validity (integer or enum bounds) 3. model_dir MUST exist on filesystem 4. max_new_tokens bounds 5. temperature bounds 6. top_k bounds 7. top_p bounds 8. repetition_penalty bounds 9. vsock_cid validity 10. vsock_port validity 11. max_message_bytes bounds 12. pgov.cosine_similarity_threshold bounds 13. (any additional constraint surfaced in entrypoint.py validate_runtime_config)
FLOOR: ≥ 6 of 13. CEILING: 13 (preferred under mature-not-minimal).
For each constraint covered, add one test that: - Writes a TOML file to tmp_path with the targeted field set to an invalid value. - Calls the AO's validate_runtime_config() equivalent (read entrypoint.py for the exact method name and signature). - Asserts: (ok is False) AND (fingerprint["code"] starts with a recognizable AO error prefix) AND (the error code matches the constraint being tested).
Each test MUST assert the specific error code string — not merely that validation failed. Failing-closed with the wrong code is still a bug.
Read before writing: services/assistant_orchestrator/src/entrypoint.py (validate_runtime_config method OR equivalent config-validation path; read in full to enumerate the exact constraint checks) services/assistant_orchestrator/src/constants.py (to verify expected value ranges) services/policy_agent/tests/test_entrypoint.py (for an equivalent config-validation test pattern, if one exists post-EA-1)
COMPLETION REPORT REQUIREMENT: State exactly how many of the 13 were covered, list by name the constraints covered, list by name any constraints NOT covered with a one-sentence rationale (e.g., "covered indirectly by existing test X", "requires production change to make testable — deferred"). The residual count MUST appear in the commit message as well.

item.source: TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md §Coverage Map — AO entrypoint.py — HEARTBEAT handling
item.title: Add AO entrypoint.py HEARTBEAT message handling test
item.detail:
File: services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_entrypoint.py (MODIFY) Class: TestAssistantOrchestratorHeartbeat (CREATE NEW CLASS)
Add test: test_handle_connection_dispatches_heartbeat Construct or mock the AO _handle_connection path to receive a framed HEARTBEAT message (read entrypoint.py _handle_connection to identify the exact message dispatch — HANDSHAKE and PROMPT are already covered; HEARTBEAT is not). Feed the HEARTBEAT message through the handler. Assert: the expected HEARTBEAT response payload is emitted (read the production code to determine what the response shape is; do not invent it). Assert: no exception is raised. Assert: the connection remains open after HEARTBEAT (HEARTBEAT is a liveness probe, not a terminator).
Read before writing: services/assistant_orchestrator/src/entrypoint.py (_handle_connection) shared/ipc/protocol.py (MessageType.HEARTBEAT if defined; HEARTBEAT encoder if one exists — the audit notes encode_handshake_request, encode_handshake_response, encode_prompt_request, encode_stream_token, encode_pgov_result, encode_generation_complete exist) services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_entrypoint.py (existing HANDSHAKE and PROMPT test patterns — mirror exactly)
Mocking strategy: follow the existing handshake-dispatch test fixture pattern in test_entrypoint.py. Do not open a real socket.

item.source: TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md §Coverage Map — AO entrypoint.py — stop() not isolated
item.title: Add AO entrypoint.py stop() isolation test
item.detail:
File: services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_entrypoint.py (MODIFY) Class: TestAssistantOrchestratorStopIsolation (CREATE NEW CLASS, or add to an existing non-start-path class)
Add test: test_stop_when_not_running_does_not_raise Construct an AO service instance with a minimal valid config (use the existing _write_minimal_config test helper, or author one if missing). Do NOT call start(). Call service.stop(). Assert: no exception is raised. Assert: service.running is False (or service._running if that is the attribute).
Add test: test_stop_after_start_completes_cleanly If a full start()-then-stop() flow is feasible with existing test mocks (gpu_inference, pgov detector, and listener all mocked), add a second test that starts the service, then stops it, and asserts clean shutdown signal (running flag is False, no lingering listener state). If the start() path cannot be exercised without real GPU/model load, skip this second test and note in the completion report.
Read before writing: services/assistant_orchestrator/src/entrypoint.py (stop() method) services/policy_agent/tests/test_entrypoint.py (test_stop_when_not_running_does_not_raise from EA-1, if merged — mirror the pattern)

item.source: TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md §Coverage Map — AO circuit_breaker.py — ADEQUATE (with gaps)
item.title: Add circuit_breaker.py over-limit-token, simultaneous-trip, and new_request() reset tests
item.detail:
File: services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_circuit_breaker.py (MODIFY)
Add three tests:
test_token_breaker_over_limit_trips: At HEAD, tests cover at-limit and under-limit cases but not strictly OVER the limit (e.g., current_tokens == OUTPUT_TOKEN_CAP + 1). Construct a TokenBreaker with OUTPUT_TOKEN_CAP and feed an over-cap value. Assert it trips. Assert the tripped state exposes the expected preemption reason (read the production class for the exact enum / string).
test_simultaneous_token_and_depth_cap_trip: Construct both the token breaker and the tool-call depth breaker in a single circuit-breaker context (read circuit_breaker.py for the exact orchestration class — it may be CircuitBreaker, BreakerSet, or similar). Feed values that trip BOTH caps in the same request. Assert that BOTH trip flags are set and that the reported preemption reason is either the combined reason, the first- tripped reason, or whichever the production code specifies. Test documents the current behavior — do NOT assume priority.
test_new_request_resets_breaker_state: Construct a circuit breaker, trip it (either via the token cap or depth cap), verify the tripped state, call new_request() (or the equivalent reset method — read the production class), and assert that all breaker counters and tripped flags are reset to their initial values.
Read before writing: services/assistant_orchestrator/src/circuit_breaker.py (in full) services/assistant_orchestrator/src/constants.py (OUTPUT_TOKEN_CAP, TOOL_CALL_DEPTH_CAP) services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_circuit_breaker.py (existing 7 tests in 3 classes — mirror the setup style)

item.source: TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md §Coverage Map — AO pgov.py — Untested PII Patterns (CREDIT_CARD, HEX_SECRET)
item.title: Add pgov.py CREDIT_CARD and HEX_SECRET PII pattern tests
item.detail:
File: services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_pgov.py (MODIFY) Class: TestPGOVPIIPatterns (existing class — read test_pgov.py to confirm exact class name; if different, add to whichever class covers the other 7 PII patterns)
Add test: test_pii_pattern_credit_card_detected Feed an output string containing a representative credit-card-like number (e.g., "4111 1111 1111 1111" or "4111-1111-1111-1111"; use a Luhn-valid test card number or a clearly-synthetic 16-digit sequence — do NOT use a real cardholder number). Run the PII detection stage of PGOV. Assert: the CREDIT_CARD pattern matches and triggers the denial path. Assert: result.approved is False. Assert: the denial reason code references CREDIT_CARD (or the production equivalent; read pgov.py _PII_PATTERNS to identify the exact label).
Add test: test_pii_pattern_hex_secret_detected Feed an output string containing a 32+ character hex string (e.g., "a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4"). Run PII detection. Assert: the HEX_SECRET pattern matches and triggers the denial path. Assert: result.approved is False. Assert: the denial reason code references HEX_SECRET.
Read before writing: services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py (_PII_PATTERNS list — confirm CREDIT_CARD and HEX_SECRET regexes; author test inputs that match) services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_pgov.py (existing 7 PII pattern tests — mirror exactly)

item.title: Create test_constants_ao.py with direct assertion tests for all AO behavioral constants
item.detail:
File to CREATE: services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/test_constants_ao.py Class: TestAssistantOrchestratorConstants
Author one test per constant value (or a single parametrized test). Required coverage — read services/assistant_orchestrator/src/constants.py for the exact value of each:
test_pgov_cosine_threshold: from services.assistant_orchestrator.src.constants import PGOV_COSINE_THRESHOLD assert PGOV_COSINE_THRESHOLD == 0.85
test_output_token_cap: assert OUTPUT_TOKEN_CAP == <value in constants.py>
test_tool_call_depth_cap: assert TOOL_CALL_DEPTH_CAP == <value in constants.py>
test_default_generation_params: assert DEFAULT_TEMPERATURE == <value> assert DEFAULT_TOP_K == <value> (include DEFAULT_TOP_P, DEFAULT_REPETITION_PENALTY if present)
test_ao_device_constant: assert AO_DEVICE == "GPU" (post-ADR-011 value; re-exported from shared)
test_stale_reexports_mapping: Note: the audit flags NPU_PRIORITY and NPU_KV_CACHE_PERSISTS as stale re-exports retained for schema compatibility. Assert their CURRENT values to pin the mapping to the GPU-era equivalents. Do NOT rename — stale renaming is EA-5 scope.
Any additional behavioral constant present in constants.py that gates operational behavior. Read the file end-to-end; constants-test coverage is comprehensive per mature-not-minimal.
Rationale: A regression changing PGOV_COSINE_THRESHOLD from 0.85 to 0.80 or flipping AO_DEVICE back to "NPU" would not be caught by any existing unit test. Direct constant-value assertions provide a diagnostic signal with the first regression change.

item.title: Create test_constants_sr.py with direct assertion tests for all SR behavioral constants
item.detail:
File to CREATE: services/semantic_router/tests/test_constants_sr.py Class: TestSemanticRouterConstants
Author one test per constant value. Required coverage — read services/semantic_router/src/constants.py for the exact value of each:
test_confidence_threshold: from services.semantic_router.src.constants import CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD assert CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD == 0.50
test_confidence_margin: from services.semantic_router.src.constants import CONFIDENCE_MARGIN assert CONFIDENCE_MARGIN == 0.04
test_any_other_behavioral_constants: Read constants.py end-to-end. Anchor every value present (intent labels, embedding model identifier, default fallback intent, etc.) with an explicit assertion. Mature-not-minimal: full coverage preferred.
Rationale: CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD and CONFIDENCE_MARGIN are the dual-gate production defaults. A value regression (e.g., 0.50 → 0.05) would only be detected when classification behavior visibly changes — too indirect for fleet-era ops.

item.source: TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md §Assertion Quality — ui_shell — Assignment Posing As Assertion in test_hide_sets_display_none
item.title: Fix pgov_display.py hide() assignment-posing-as-assertion bug
item.detail:
File: services/ui_shell/tests/test_pgov_display.py (MODIFY) Test: TestPGOVPanelLogic::test_hide_sets_display_none (lines 118-121 at HEAD 29cea32)
Current state (buggy): def test_hide_sets_display_none(self, panel: PGOVPanel) -> None: panel.display_denial(_make_denial()) panel.hide() panel.styles.display = "none" # Verify the assignment happened
The final line is an ASSIGNMENT, not an assertion. The test passes regardless of whether hide() actually resets styles.display. A regression in which hide() fails to set styles.display = "none" is not caught.
Required fix: Replace the assignment with a real assertion: assert panel.styles.display == "none"
The sibling test test_display_denial_sets_block_display already uses this correct idiom. Mirror it.
NOTE ON SCOPE: This fix touches services/ui_shell/tests/test_pgov_display.py — not an AO test file. The continuation XML §5 EA-2 scope labels this item as "AO pgov_display.py" but the actual production module services/ui_shell/src/pgov_display.py lives in ui_shell, and its test is in services/ui_shell/tests/test_pgov_display.py. EA-2 owns the fix per SDV §5.1 item 2. This is a one-line assertion correction, not a structural move — scope-appropriate for EA-2.
Read before writing: services/ui_shell/tests/test_pgov_display.py (lines 118-121, and the sibling test test_display_denial_sets_block_display for the correct assertion idiom) services/ui_shell/src/pgov_display.py (PGOVPanel.hide() method — confirm the assertion target is correct)
No new tests are required for WI-10 — this is a bug fix to an existing test. The net test count may not change from this WI, but the assertion quality does.

file.test_functions: test_pgov_cosine_threshold, test_output_token_cap, test_tool_call_depth_cap, test_default_generation_params, test_ao_device_constant, test_stale_reexports_mapping (plus any additional behavioral constants discovered while reading constants.py)
test_confidence_threshold, test_confidence_margin, test_any_other_behavioral_constants (catch-all; split as constants.py warrants)

file.changes: WI-3: new class TestAssistantOrchestratorConfigValidation with ≥ 6 (all 13 preferred) parametrized or per-constraint tests asserting (ok is False) AND specific error_code. WI-4: new class TestAssistantOrchestratorHeartbeat with test_handle_connection_dispatches_heartbeat. WI-5: new class TestAssistantOrchestratorStopIsolation with test_stop_when_not_running_does_not_raise and optional test_stop_after_start_completes_cleanly.

WI-6: three new tests — test_token_breaker_over_limit_trips, test_simultaneous_token_and_depth_cap_trip, test_new_request_resets_breaker_state (placed in the existing class whose pattern most closely matches, or a new class if none fits).
WI-7: two new tests — test_pii_pattern_credit_card_detected, test_pii_pattern_hex_secret_detected (added to the existing class covering the other 7 PII patterns).

WI-10: fix test_hide_sets_display_none — replace `panel.styles.display = "none"` assignment on the final line with `assert panel.styles.display == "none"` real assertion. No other changes to this file.

Add Entry 52 (or next free entry — verify at commit time): Task 8 / EA-2: Assistant Orchestrator + Semantic Router Test Hardening — COMPLETE. Format: follow the existing entry format exactly (see Entry 50, Entry 51 for reference if EA-1's entry has merged). Include: branch name, commit hash, net new test count, baseline before/after, date, WI disposition summary, residual AO config-validation constraint count (WI-3).

deliverable.naming_conventions: Test class names: PascalCase, descriptive (TestPGOVLeakageThresholdBoundary, TestAssistantOrchestratorConfigValidation, etc.). Test function names: snake_case, starts with test_, describes the behavior being tested. No numbered prefixes on section headers in any test file or comprehension gate (L-12). No extra sections or test classes beyond what is listed in this deliverable (L-12). Do NOT rename any existing class, function, or file — stale naming cleanup is EA-5 scope.
deliverable.ledger_entry_reserved: 52 (verify next free entry at commit time; may advance to 53+ if other sprint activity has consumed it)

negative_constraints.constraint: DO NOT modify any file outside tests/, conftest.py, docs/, or pyproject.toml. If a production code change is required to unblock a test, STOP, document the blocker in your completion report, and wait for SDO direction. Do not make the change unilaterally.

DO NOT rename stale NPU identifiers in AO, SR, or any cross-service test file (e.g., NPU_PRIORITY, NPU_KV_CACHE_PERSISTS re-exports, _make_npu_stub helpers, TestPipelineWithNPUStub class names). Your tests may reference these identifiers (to add coverage) but must not rename them. Renaming is EA-5 scope.
DO NOT author EA-1, EA-3, EA-4, or EA-5 work. Only assistant_orchestrator and semantic_router tests (plus the one ui_shell test-file bug-fix in WI-10) are in scope for this EA.

DO NOT add ISS-1 (AO speculative decoding), ISS-2 (think tags in TUI), or ISS-3 (PA stop-token mismatch) tests. These are tracked issues requiring production code changes and are explicitly deferred per SDV §13.1 and Sprint 8 out-of-scope policy.

DO NOT add integration tests requiring a live Hyper-V VM, real vsock socket, real GPU inference, or real Qwen3 model load. All new tests must be unit-scoped and runnable under the REGRESSION pytest scope (no slow marker, no integration marker).
DO NOT author tests for USE-CASE-002, USE-CASE-003, or USE-CASE-005 features. Only USE-CASE-004 (AO) and the semantic router cluster that feeds it are in scope.
DO NOT use parallel EA execution. EA-3 cannot begin until EA-2 has merged. SDO enforces sequential ordering per SDV §5.2.

DO NOT add sections, headers, or content that is not part of the deliverable contract in Section 5 (L-12 compliance). Do not rename required sections. Do not add numbered prefixes to section headers in your comprehension gate response.
DO NOT author a retroactive Sprint 7 SCR or SWAGR. Sprint 7 closed under pre-DEC-15 rules; its audit output is the input to Sprint 8 and does not need retroactive reporting.
DO NOT create a root-level conftest.py. If fixture sharing is required, create services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/conftest.py or services/semantic_router/tests/conftest.py only.

DO NOT invent error codes, response shapes, or reason strings. Every assertion against a production value must match the actual production code at HEAD 29cea32 (or current main at pickup). Read the production file; do not guess.

quality_gate.gate: After authoring all tests, verify no import errors: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI .venv\Scripts\python -c "import services.assistant_orchestrator.tests.test_pgov_boundaries" .venv\Scripts\python -c "import services.assistant_orchestrator.tests.test_constants_ao" .venv\Scripts\python -c "import services.semantic_router.tests.test_dual_gate_thresholds" .venv\Scripts\python -c "import services.semantic_router.tests.test_constants_sr" No ImportError or ModuleNotFoundError is acceptable. Any import failure indicates a stale reference or missing __init__.py and must be resolved before TEST gate.

Run the REGRESSION test suite: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI .venv\Scripts\pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q PASS criteria: test count MUST NOT decrease below the preceding branch's count. At authoring time the post-EA-1 baseline is approximately 813 passed, 2 skipped (EA-1 added ~18 tests to the 755 Sprint 8 start baseline). EA MUST re-check the exact baseline at pickup by running the suite on main BEFORE creating the EA-2 branch. The acceptance rule is: post-EA-2 count ≥ post-EA-1 count. Record both numbers in the commit message. Any new test must appear in the count (count should increase by ≥ 15). No pre-existing tests may regress.

Verify no production files were modified: git diff main...feature/p5-task8-ea2-ao-sr-hardening --name-only | grep -vE "tests|conftest|docs|pyproject" Expected output: EMPTY (zero lines). If ANY production file appears in the filtered diff, the EA has violated Sprint 8's mandate. STOP and report the violation in the completion report before doing anything else. Do NOT attempt to "undo" the change without SDO direction — the branch is evidence.

verification.description: These are verbatim terminal commands the Lead Architect (non-developer) can run to confirm success. Run from C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI in PowerShell.

step.expected: Working directory set to the BlarAI repo root. No output expected.

Most recent commit on main should be the EA-2 merge commit (Co-Lead auto-merges after EA-2 completes). Commit subject should begin: test(task8/ea2): AO + semantic_router boundary + isolation + constants hardening — followed by "N new tests, baseline X → Y".

After merge: empty (the branch is merged; working tree is clean). Before merge (on the feature branch): only test files under services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/, services/semantic_router/tests/, services/ui_shell/tests/test_pgov_display.py (the WI-10 fix), and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. NO production source files under services/*/src/ or shared/.
At least (post-EA-1 baseline) passed, 2 skipped. The exact count should be higher than the post-EA-1 count by at least 15 tests. The exact numbers appear in the EA-2 commit message (X → Y).

step.command: git diff main...feature/p5-task8-ea2-ao-sr-hardening --name-only | findstr /v /R "tests conftest docs pyproject"
step.expected: Empty output (no production files in the diff). This is the ORACLE gate verification. If any line appears, it is a Sprint 8 mandate violation.
All AO and SR tests pass, including all new WI-1 through WI-9 tests. The WI-10 fix is under ui_shell, verified by V-4 above.

commit_template.format: test(task8/ea2): AO + semantic_router boundary + isolation + constants hardening — {N} new tests, baseline {X} → {Y}
commit_template.example: test(task8/ea2): AO + semantic_router boundary + isolation + constants hardening — 21 new tests, baseline 813 → 834
commit_template.rules: - N = exact count of new tests added by this EA (verify with `pytest --co -q` diff between main@pre-branch and feature/p5-task8-ea2-ao-sr-hardening). - X = total passed count on main BEFORE this EA's branch was created (run the regression suite on main at pickup time and record the number). - Y = total passed count AFTER this EA's changes merge (run the regression suite on the feature branch after all commits). - Commit only files under services/assistant_orchestrator/tests/, services/semantic_router/tests/, services/ui_shell/tests/test_pgov_display.py, and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. - Do NOT commit any production source files. - Do NOT squash into a single commit if multiple logical commits are clearer — Co-Lead accepts multi-commit feature branches.

mature_not_minimal.directive:
EA-2 is authorized to address directly adjacent findings discovered during work — even if not pre-listed in docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md — provided each new item adds ≤ 1 hour of additional work (per SDV §5.3).
Examples of in-scope adjacent work: - While adding WI-3 config-validation tests, you discover a fourteenth constraint that the audit missed. Add it and cover it. - While reading circuit_breaker.py for WI-6, you notice a reset-while-running edge case that has no test. Add it if ≤ 1 hour. - While adding WI-8 AO constants tests, you realize a cross-referenced shared constant (e.g. from shared/constants.py) should also be value-anchored in the AO constants test. Add it. - While fixing WI-10, you notice a sibling test in the same class has a similar assignment-posing-as-assertion bug. Fix it.
Items EXCEEDING the 1-hour cap per new adjacent item: - Document in your completion report as a new finding with its estimated effort. - Co-Lead will open a new Vikunja task for the next sprint (per SDV §5.3). - Do NOT absorb them silently into this EA.
The AO config-validation coverage target specifically (WI-3): the floor is ≥ 6 of 13; the mature-not-minimal preference is ALL 13. Residual uncovered constraints (if any) MUST be named and justified in the completion report.
This is NOT a license for unbounded scope. EA-2 addresses AO and SR ONLY. PA tests are EA-1 scope; UI gateway and UI shell app.py / session_panel.py are EA-3 scope (the one WI-10 ui_shell test-file line-level fix is specifically called out in SDV §5.1 item 2 as EA-2 scope); shared/launcher is EA-4 scope; structural cleanup is EA-5 scope.

file.sections: §5 task8_context ea_decomposition EA-2 block (lines ~227-253), §6 lessons_learned L-12 L-13 L-15, §7 ea_prompt_guidelines
file.reason: Authoritative SDO continuation; EA-2 scope contract and L-rule requirements

file.sections: §5.1 item 2 (EA-2 scope), §5.2 Out-of-scope, §5.3 Edge cases and mature-not-minimal 1-hour cap, §4 Success Criteria, §13.1 Deferred issues
file.reason: Strategic alignment baseline — EA-2 scope must align with SDV §5.1 item 2

file.sections: §Coverage Map assistant_orchestrator, §Coverage Map semantic_router, §Boundary Violations AO (0.85), §Boundary Violations SR (0.50 / 0.04 / 0.03), §Assertion Quality ui_shell (hide assignment bug), §Stale Test Inventory AO and SR
file.reason: Complete input specification for EA-2 scope — read the AO and SR sections in full

file.sections: marker taxonomy (unit / integration / slow), pre-existing skip list, baseline reporting rules
file.reason: Authoritative marker taxonomy and test placement rules

file.sections: Entry 50, Entry 51 (EA-1) if merged, latest entries for format reference
file.reason: Required for Entry 52 (or next free entry) — follow the existing entry format exactly
Required for WI-1 (PGOV leakage threshold), WI-7 (CREDIT_CARD + HEX_SECRET PII patterns) — read PGOVPipeline leakage path and _PII_PATTERNS list
Required for WI-6 — read CircuitBreaker / BreakerSet orchestration, TokenBreaker over-limit path, new_request() reset method in full
Required for WI-3 (validate_runtime_config constraint enumeration), WI-4 (_handle_connection HEARTBEAT dispatch), WI-5 (stop() method)
Required for WI-1, WI-6, WI-8 — verify PGOV_COSINE_THRESHOLD=0.85, OUTPUT_TOKEN_CAP, TOOL_CALL_DEPTH_CAP, DEFAULT_* generation params, AO_DEVICE, stale NPU re-exports
Required for WI-2 — read classify() or equivalent dual-gate logic to identify centroid/embedding injection seams
Required for WI-2, WI-9 — verify CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD=0.50, CONFIDENCE_MARGIN=0.04, and enumerate any other behavioral constants
Required for WI-2 — centroid structures and intent taxonomy if relevant to the dual-gate boundary tests
Required for WI-10 — confirm PGOVPanel.hide() sets styles.display = 'none' so the corrected assertion target is valid
Required for WI-1 (mirror test_leakage_with_injected_detector pattern), WI-7 (mirror existing 7 PII pattern tests)
Required for WI-6 — understand existing 7 tests in 3 classes and mirror setup style
Required for WI-3, WI-4, WI-5 — understand existing HANDSHAKE + PROMPT dispatch tests and config-validation patterns
Required for WI-2 — understand existing router test patterns and mocking style
Required for WI-10 — the target of the one-line assertion-correction fix, and the correct idiom to mirror
Reference only — full milestone tracking; EA does not edit
